commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=e5f13da3945f9a9d90bee3db7c926e50ebcd1186)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a ClickClack troubleshooting entry that tells users to rebuild `clickclack-proxy` and restart the gateway for CSRF 403 errors through a managed proxy.

PR surface: Docs +10. Total +10 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: no. end-to-end current-main runtime reproduction was run for the mobile/Tailscale proxy path. The PR defect is source-reproducible: the added snippet restarts the Gateway, while the linked report and current source search point to a separate `clickclack-proxy` process with no checked-in lifecycle hook.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainers need to confirm the canonical ClickClack proxy owner and service restart path before this workaround can become authoritative docs.

target.title: docs(clickclack): add CSRF 403 troubleshooting for managed proxy (#98362)
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where users accessing ClickClack through the OpenClaw-managed proxy over Tailscale Serve see `cross-site session requests are not allowed` (403 CSRF) errors. The proxy binary can become stale after OpenClaw updates.

## Why This Change Was Made

The `clickclack-proxy` Go reverse proxy source is not version-controlled in this repository. The definitive fix (proper Origin/Host header forwarding in the proxy) belongs in the installer/tooling repository. This PR adds a troubleshooting entry so affected users can resolve the issue immediately by rebuilding the proxy.

## User Impact

Users hitting CSRF 403 errors through the managed proxy now have actionable recovery steps in the official docs.

## Evidence

- Issue reporter confirmed rebuild fixed the problem (see #98362)
- Direct ClickClack access works; only the proxied path is affected
